title: Cabinet assures nation of the monitoring of new COVID variant
description: NATIONAL NEWS - Cabinet has assured South...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"NATIONAL NEWS - Cabinet has assured South Africans that government, through the Department of Health, is closely monitoring the emergence of a new COVID-19 variant, known as Nimbus or NB.1.8.1.","html":"NATIONAL NEWS - Cabinet has assured South Africans that government, through the Department of Health, is closely monitoring the emergence of a new COVID-19 variant, known as Nimbus or NB.1.8.1."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“Cabinet calls on all citizens to ensure good hygiene practices, including avoiding unnecessary hand shaking, washing hands with soap, covering the mouth when coughing, using a mask when one has flu and staying home when one is not feeling well,” Minister in the Presidency Khumbudzo Ntshavheni said on Thursday, in Cape Town.","html":"“Cabinet calls on all citizens to ensure good hygiene practices, including avoiding unnecessary hand shaking, washing hands with soap, covering the mouth when coughing, using a mask when one has flu and staying home when one is not feeling well,” Minister in the Presidency Khumbudzo Ntshavheni said on Thursday, in Cape Town."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"This as the virus is associated with a rise in cases in certain regions of Asia."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“During the COVID-19 pandemic, we demonstrated that through these simple measures we could reduce the spread of respiratory illness.”","html":"“During the COVID-19 pandemic, we demonstrated that through these simple measures we could reduce the spread of respiratory illness.”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The Minister was briefing members of the media on the outcomes of the Cabinet meeting yesterday, Wednesday, 11 June.","html":"The Minister was briefing members of the media on the outcomes of the Cabinet meeting yesterday, Wednesday, 11 June."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"According to Health Minister, Dr Aaron Motsoaledi, South Africa has robust surveillance systems that is managed by the National Institute for Communicable Diseases (NICD).","html":"According to Health Minister, Dr Aaron Motsoaledi, South Africa has robust surveillance systems that is managed by the National Institute for Communicable Diseases (NICD)."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The NICD manages a comprehensive sentinel surveillance programme that systematically tests for key respiratory viruses, including SARS-CoV-2, influenza, and RSV.","html":"The NICD manages a comprehensive sentinel surveillance programme that systematically tests for key respiratory viruses, including SARS-CoV-2, influenza, and RSV."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Currently, data show very low SARS-CoV-2 activity. -SAnews.gov.za","html":"Currently, data show very low SARS-CoV-2 activity. -SAnews.gov.za"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: Cabinet assures nation of the monitoring of new COVID variant
